packageRules.description: Auto-merge non-0.x patch/minor once CI (lint/typecheck/test/build + audits) is green.
0.x deps: any bump can break; never auto-merge.
Group Radix UI primitives into one PR.
Majors always require human review + migration.

eslint 10 is blocked by eslint-plugin-react, pulled in transitively via eslint-config-next (which depends on eslint-plugin-react ^7.37.0). Its latest release, 7.37.5, peer-supports eslint up to ^9.7 only and calls the rule-context API context.getFilename(), which eslint 10 removed (now context.filename). Flat config is already in use (web/eslint.config.mjs) — that is NOT the blocker. Clears only when eslint-plugin-react ships an eslint-10-compatible release AND eslint-config-next adopts it.

@types/node major must track the Node runtime major, currently 24 (engines.node >=24, .nvmrc, ci.yml node-version, web/Dockerfile node:24). Don't let @types/node run ahead of the runtime; bump both together when Node moves.

Known breaking-within-minor packages (2026-06 audit found code-migration needs in minor/0.x bumps). react-hook-form/resolvers and react-query were migrated in Stage 3 but stay flagged (future minors have shipped breaking type changes). openapi-typescript needs a matching scripts/build-types-shim.py update before bumping past 7.4.
Rust HTTP-client trio must move together (oauth2/openidconnect pin reqwest 0.12).

yaml is not a direct dep; it exists only as an override-only security pin forcing the legacy swagger2openapi/oas-* docs toolchain (which needs the yaml 1.x API) to a safe 1.x version. yaml v2 is a breaking rewrite that would break docusaurus-plugin-openapi-docs. Keep the pin on 1.x; still allow 1.x security patches.
sea-orm pins sqlx (sea-orm 1.1.x → sqlx 0.8.x). Never bump sqlx standalone — it won't co-resolve. Move as one PR; sqlx 0.9 waits for sea-orm 2.x.
apalis pins the redis crate (apalis-redis 0.6.x → redis 0.27.x). The redis crate never bumps standalone — it moves with apalis/apalis-redis.

RustCrypto digest/rand ecosystem moves in lockstep — digest 0.11 (sha2 0.11 / hmac 0.13) and rand 0.10 are breaking jumps that the AEAD/signature crates (argon2, rsa, ed25519-dalek, chacha20poly1305) pin transitively. Group so they never surface as conflicting standalone bumps; needs code migration.
